lessonTitle: Splenic Sequestration in Sickle Cell Disease

items.html: Identify splenic sequestration as a medical emergency
Review presentation of patients with SCD and splenic sequestration
List appropriate management strategies for splenic sequestration

slides.title: What are 3 possible differential diagnoses for Leila?

data.question: What are 3 possible differential diagnoses for Leila?
data.feedback_correct: Based on Leila's case, Sepsis , Splenic sequestration and Severe anemia could be our possible diagnoses.

data.html: Spleen is prone to injury in SCD - slow and open microcirculation favors deoxygenation, sickling and adhesion. Splenic dysfunction starts very early in life, mostly before the first birthday. Auto-splenectomy is completed by 5 years in most patients with HbSS and HbSβ0.

slides.title: Presentation of Splenic Sequestration in SCD

items.html: One of the earliest life-threatening complications of SCD.
Noted as early as 5 weeks; median age is 1.4 years of age.
Majority of events (75%) will occur before 2 years of age.
Lifelong prevalence 7-30%.
Associated clinical event in approximately 50% of patients.
50-75% risk of recurrence.
Splenic sequestration is a medical emergency.
Early detection and parental education are key.
Urgent restoration of circulating blood volume is essential.
Treat with small volume simple red blood cell transfusions.
Risk of hyperviscosity with large-volume transfusions.
Consultation to ICU if hemodynamically stable.
Consideration of elective splenectomy by primary SCD team: After one episode of acute and/or life-threatening splenic sequestration Or recurrent subacute or chronic sequestrations
Vaccination pre-splenectomy.
Penicillin prophylaxis post splenectomy.

slides.title: What are the appropriate management strategies for splenic sequestration?

data.question: What are the appropriate management strategies for splenic sequestration?
options: Consultation to ICU if hemodynamically stable
Small volume simple red blood cell transfusions
correct_answer: Consultation to ICU if hemodynamically stable
Small volume simple red blood cell transfusions
data.feedback_correct: Here are some appropriate management strategies for splenic sequestration: Consultation to ICU if hemodynamically stable Small volume simple red blood cell transfusions Penicillin prophylaxis post splenectomy

slides.title: True or False? Drag to categorize the following statements

data.question: Splenic sequestration can occur as early as 5 weeks of age.
data.feedback_correct: True: Splenic sequestration can occur as early as 5 weeks of age.

data.question: Patients with HbSC are at higher risk for splenic sequestration than patients with HbSS or HbSB0.
data.feedback_correct: False: Patients with HbSC are at higher risk for splenic sequestration than patients with HbSS or HbSB0.

items.html: Splenic sequestration is a medical emergency!
Characterized by the presence of an enlarging spleen and a sudden drop in hemoglobin concentration..
Isolated or associated with other complications like infection or pain crisis.
Parental/caregiver education and use of hydroxyurea can help preserve spleen function and allow for early identification of the need to seek care.
Brousse, V., Buffet, P. and Rees, D. (2014), The spleen and sickle cell disease: the sick(led) spleen. Br J Haematol, 166: 165-176. https://doi.org/10.1111/bjh.12950

Brousse, V., Elie, C., Benkerrou, M., Odièvre, M.-H., Lesprit, E., Bernaudin, F., Grimaud, M., Guitton, C., Quinet, B., Dangiolo, S. and de Montalembert, M. (2012), Acute splenic sequestration crisis in sickle cell disease: cohort study of 190 paediatric patients. British Journal of Haematology, 156: 643-648. https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1365-2141.2011.08999.x

Kane I, Kumar A, Atalla E, et al. Splenic Sequestration Crisis. [Updated 2023 Jun 5]. In: StatPearls [Internet]. Treasure Island (FL): StatPearls Publishing; 2024 Jan-. Available from: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK553164/
